ORDER OVERRULING CAPSTAR COMMERCIAL REAL ESTATE SERVICE'S, LTD'S AND CCRES, LTD'S DEMURRER
ORDER OVERRULIGN CASSIDY TURLEY, INC.'S DEMURRER

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS CAPSTAR COMMERCIAL REAL ESTATE SERVICES, LTD. AND CCRES, LTD.'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT IS OVERRULED. THE COMPLAINT SUFFICIENTLY PLEADS THE DISCOVERY RULE (19-21) AND THE ALLEGATIONS DO NOT SHOW THAT THE CLAIMS ARE TIME BARRED. THE COMPLAINT IS NOT SO UNCERTAIN THAT DEFENDANT CANNOT REASONABLY RESPOND. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY C. VAUGHN, CSR # 6185

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT CASSIDY TURLEY, INC.'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT IS OVERRULED. EVEN IF THE COURT TAKES JUDICIAL NOTICE OF THE EXISTENCE OF THE REDACTED ASSET PURCHASE AGREEMENT, THE COURT CANNOT ACCEPT THE TRUTH OF THE RECITATIONS IN THAT DOCUMENT. PARAGRAPH 5 OF THE COMPLAINT ALLEGES THAT CAPSTAR AND CASSIDY TURLEY MERGED AND THE COURT MUST ACCEPT THE TRUTH OF THAT ALLEGATION FOR PURPOSES OF THE DEMURRER. DEFENDANT SHOULD BRING A MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT TO RESOLVE THIS FACTUAL ISSUE. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY C. VAUGHN, CSR # 6185

MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Nov-19-2013 Continued To Apr-07-14 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Jan-21-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: MATTHEW TROUGHTON, ESQ., 415.774.3250, FOR PLAINTIFF DIGITAL REALTY TRUST ET AL.
THOMAS D'AMATO, ESQ., 415.788.1900, FOR DEFENDANT CAPSTAR COMMERCIAL REAL ESTATE SERVICES, LTD. ET AL.

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling: DEFENDANTS CASSIDY TURLEY, INC., CASSIDY TURLEY COMMERCIAL REAL ESTATE SERVICES, CAPSTAR COMMERCIAL REAL ESTATE SERVICES, LTD. AND CCRES, LTD.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS Denied. When a defendant seeks summary judgment based on an affirmative defense, the defendant must present evidence that, if uncontradicted, "would constitute a preponderance of the evidence" that the affirmative defense can be established. (Kids' Universe v. In2Labs (2002) 95 Cal.App.4th 870, 879.) The evidence submitted does not establish, by the preponderance of the evidence, that Plaintiffs had a "suspicion of wrongdoing." (See Jolly v. Eli Lilly & Co. (1988) 44 Cal.3d 1103, 1111.) Rather, the evidence shows that Plaintiffs had difficulties getting paperwork from Krahl Construction in a timely fashion and that Krahl Construction made errors on occasion on the paperwork it did submit. (See Evidence in support of Undisputed Fact ("UF") nos. 26-44.) Alternatively, even if Defendants had met their burden, Plaintiffs have submitted evidence that establishes a triable issue of material fact as to whether Plaintiffs had a suspicion of wrongdoing and therefore inquiry notice, or whether Tom Stratford's inquiries were to fulfill his "'to push for timely and complete paperwork." (Declaration of John Thomas Stratford, 8-10.) Defendant met its burden of showing that Defendant Cassidy Turley, Inc. ("Cassidy") did not assume all the liabilities of Defendant Capstar Commercial Real Estate Services, Ltd. ("Capstar"). The burden then shifted to Plaintiffs. Plaintiffs met their burden of showing a triable issue of material fact exists as to whether a de facto merger occurred. Defendants are correct that Franklin v. USX Corp. stands for the proposition that when there is a cash purchase of assets, adequate consideration is the critical factor in determining whether a de facto merger has occurred. ((2001) 87 Cal.App.4th 615, 621.) However, as discussed in Franklin, where assets are purchased with stock, a de facto merger can be found. (Id. at 626.) Here, the evidence shows that Cassidy paid both stock and cash for virtually all of Capstar's assets. (UF no. 71; Troughton Decl., Ex. 5, Depo of Florent, 51:10-16.) Further, the cash was not paid to the entity itself, but directly to the limited partners of Capstar, outside of the reach of any potential unsecured creditors. (Troughton Decl., Ex. 5, Depo of Florent, 34:5-14; 59:21-25.) Therefore, there was not a cash purchase of assets made directly to the entity, as in Franklin. As such, there is a triable issue of material fact as to whether a de facto merger took place. Order signed in open court. Judge: Ernest Goldsmith, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR5976

Amended Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Severing And/Or Bifurcating Into Separate Trials
Off Calendar Jan-16-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: MATHEW TROUGHTON, ESQ., 415.434.9100, FOR PLAINTIFFS DIGITAL REALTY TRUST, LP ET AL.
THOMAS D'AMATO, ESQ., 415.788.1900, FOR DEFENDANTs CAPSTAR COMMERCIAL REAL ESTATE SERVICES, LTD., CCRES, LTD., CASSIDY TURLEY, INC. and CASSIDY TURLEY COMMERCIAL REAL ESTATE SERVICES.

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling: DEFENDANTs CAPSTAR COMMERCIAL REAL ESTATE SERVICES, LTD.,CCRES, LTD., CASSIDY TURLEY, INC. and CASSIDY TURLEY COMMERCIAL REAL ESTATE SERVICES' Motion For Order Severing And/Or Bifurcating Into Separate Trials is denied. Bifurcation will not serve the ends of justice because Mr. Bunnett and Mr. Patterson, who are located in Texas, are key witnesses as to the underlying liability and successor liability issues. The possibility that bifurcation will lead to them testifying twice weighs against granting the instant motion. Order signed in open court. Judge: Ernest Goldsmith, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Tony Vaughn, CSR #6185

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jan-30-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Matthew R. Troughton, Esq. of Sheppart Mullin, Tel. 415.434.9100, for plaintiff and opposing party;
No other appearance.

RULING: The court adopted its tentative ruling which was uncontested. DEFENDANTs CAPSTAR COMMERCIAL REAL ESTATE SERVICES, LTD. and CCRES, LTD.'s DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is overruled. The complaint sufficiently pleads the discovery rule (19-21) and the allegations do not show that the claims are time barred. The complaint is not so uncertain that defendant cannot reasonably respond. Order signed. Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Court Reporter: Anthony C. Vaughn, CSR # 6185

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jan-30-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Matthew R. Troughton, Esq. of Sheppart Mullin, Tel. 415.434.9100, for plaintiff and opposing party;
No other appearance.

RULING: The court adopted its tentative ruling which was not contested. DEFENDANT CASSIDY TURLEY, INC.'s DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is overruled. Even if the Court takes judicial notice of the existence of the redacted Asset Purchase Agreement, the Court cannot accept the truth of the recitations in that document. Paragraph 5 of the complaint alleges that Capstar and Cassidy Turley merged and the Court must accept the truth of that allegation for purposes of the demurrer. Defendant should bring a motion for summary judgment to resolve this factual issue. Order signed. Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Court Reporter: Anthony C. Vaughn, CSR # 6185
